A storm unlike any other the family has ever witnessed. A storm that releases abundant lightning, and sets the house on fire.A storm that takes lives.Now, seventy-four years later, a park is built on the property in commemoration of the lost souls. A playground is placed on the site where the house once stood. Something that will bring laughter and happiness.But something\u2019s not right with the park. Someone is there when no one is there. The shadows of the past begin to emerge . . .Although they died many years ago, the children still want to play.And at Lauderdale Park . . . they will.\"]"}, {"book_id": "bookid_13", "title": "Girl Made of Glass", "subtitle": "Paperback \u2013 January 24, 2023", "author": "{\"avatar\": \"https://m.media-amazon.com/images/S/amzn-author-media-prod/5av23i12r4p62nfrr8koq0blld._SY600_.jpg\", \"name\": \"Shelby Leigh\", \"about\": [\"Shelby Leigh is a mental health poet writing about self-love, insecurities, anxiety, relationships and more. args.query: SELECT book_id, title, subtitle, author, categories, details, features FROM books_info WHERE categories ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%';

exec_args.sql: SELECT book_id, title, subtitle, author, categories, details, features FROM books_info WHERE categories ILIKE '%Literature & Fiction%';

args.query: SELECT purchase_id, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating, COUNT(*) AS review_count FROM review GROUP BY purchase_id HAVING AVG(rating)=5.0;

exec_args.sql: SELECT purchase_id, AVG(rating) AS avg_rating, COUNT(*) AS review_count FROM review GROUP BY purchase_id HAVING AVG(rating)=5.0;

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd, re

# Load books query result (may be file path)
books_src = var_call_BPFdKsd0xd5CHBWlyF1sSkFd
if isinstance(books_src, str):
    with open(books_src, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        books = json.load(f)
else:
    books = books_src

reviews = var_call_krs8lqIEtmvRCVYVTg3bJ7aN

df_books = pd.DataFrame(books)
df_reviews = pd.DataFrame(reviews)

# Normalize ids: bookid_123 <-> purchaseid_123 by extracting trailing integer
pat = re.compile(r'(\d+)$')

def id_num(x):
    if x is None:
        return None
    m = pat.search(str(x))
    return int(m.group(1)) if m else None

df_books['id_num'] = df_books['book_id'].map(id_num)
df_reviews['id_num'] = df_reviews['purchase_id'].map(id_num)

df = df_books.merge(df_reviews, on='id_num', how='inner', suffixes=('_book','_rev'))

# English-language filter from details field
# Accept if details contains 'written in English' or 'Language ... English'
eng = df['details'].fillna('').str.contains(r'\bEnglish\b', case=False, regex=True)
df = df[eng].copy()

# Perfect avg rating already ensured; keep fields and sort
for col in ['avg_rating','review_count']:
    if col in df.columns:
        df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')

out = df[['title','subtitle','author','book_id','purchase_id','avg_rating','review_count']].sort_values(['title','book_id']).to_dict(orient='records')

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(out, ensure_ascii=False))

df_reviews = pd.DataFrame(reviews)

pat = re.compile(r'(\d+)$')

    if x is None:
        return None
    m = pat.search(str(x))
    return int(m.group(1)) if m else None

df_books['id_num'] = df_books['book_id'].map(id_num)
df_reviews['id_num'] = df_reviews['purchase_id'].map(id_num)
